本单元入口
整套首页
unit_21 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_21

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 365 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC SINGLE
The book's approach to modern art was hardly __________: it aimed simply to give readers a deeper understanding of prevailing perspectives in the field.The book's approach to modern art was hardly __________: it aimed simply to give readers a deeper understanding of prevailing perspectives in the field.
答案：A　revisionist
approachprevailprevailing
approach: standard unit headword | prevail: standard unit headword | prevailing: standard unit headword
#2 TC DOUBLE
The quest for new drugs to deter the aging process represents a new faith among some researchers that aging is not immutable. This (i)_______ however, is not (ii)_______. Evolutionary biologists have strong reasons to suppose that the human life span cannot be altered in any easy way.The quest for new drugs to deter the aging process represents a new faith among some researchers that aging is not immutable. This (i)_______ however, is not (ii)_______. Evolutionary biologists have strong reasons to suppose that the human life span cannot be altered in any easy way.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　optimism; fully shared
sharespanlife span
share: standard unit headword | span: standard unit headword | life span: standard unit headword
#3 Text Completion
Contrary to those who fear the impact of invasive species on native plants, the biologists contend that the threat posed to biodiversity by nonnative plants is often (i)______. For instance, a study of garlic mustard, a nonnative plant thriving in the forest, found that the garlic mustard abundance in forest plots is not (ii)______ the number of other species there.Contrary to those who fear the impact of invasive species on native plants, the biologists contend that the threat posed to biodiversity by nonnative plants is often (i)______. For instance, a study of garlic mustard, a nonnative plant thriving in the forest, found that the garlic mustard abundance in forest plots is not (ii)______ the number of other species there.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　exaggerated; related to
invasivesubtlecontend
invasive: standard unit headword | subtle: standard unit headword | contend: standard unit headword
#4 Sentence Equivalence
The physics graveyard is strewn with the skeletons of failed theories, unexplained effects, and anomalous particles that briefly ______ the research spotlight, then rapidly fade from view.The physics graveyard is strewn with the skeletons of failed theories, unexplained effects, and anomalous particles that briefly ______ the research spotlight, then rapidly fade from view.
答案：D, F　capture; secure
perpetuateintensify
perpetuate: standard unit headword | intensify: standard unit headword
#5 Text Completion
Arguments based on mere possibilities (i)______ the difficulty of discussing future technology, not least since they (ii)______ public debates on technological options. A mere possibility argument such as “Agricultural biotechnology can have serious effects on ecosystems that we still know nothing about” can be taken by opponents of the technology to be (iii)______ whereas proponents tend to dismiss it completely.Arguments based on mere possibilities (i)______ the difficulty of discussing future technology, not least since they (ii)______ public debates on technological options. A mere possibility argument such as “Agricultural biotechnology can have serious effects on ecosystems that we still know nothing about” can be taken by opponents of the technology to be (iii)______ whereas proponents tend to dismiss it completely.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　epitomize; polarize; decisive
not leastproponent
not least: standard unit headword | proponent: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　fervor; indiscriminate

#7 Sentence Equivalence
One of the vocalists who auditioned for a leading part in the local production of Sweeney Todd seemed to prefer ______ to any attempt at producing a melody: a more unpleasant voice was hard to imagine.One of the vocalists who auditioned for a leading part in the local production of Sweeney Todd seemed to prefer ______ to any attempt at producing a melody: a more unpleasant voice was hard to imagine.
答案：D, F　shrieking; caterwauling

Blank 2
答案：C, E　ignores; transform

#9 Text Completion
The humor in this play derives from its (i)______. The new production, however, inexplicably goes in the opposite direction; it is so (ii)______ that the audience does not even seem to realize that the play is supposed to be a comedy.The humor in this play derives from its (i)______. The new production, however, inexplicably goes in the opposite direction; it is so (ii)______ that the audience does not even seem to realize that the play is supposed to be a comedy.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　verbal nimbleness; plodding

Applications of the Endangered Species Act (ESA) have fared best in contexts in which habitat condition is closely linked to species condition and the cause of habitat degradation is easily identified. The achievement of ESA, in those contexts, however, have (i)______ that other uses of the act can (ii)______ that record even where such favorable conditions do not (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　fostered the misconception; duplicate; pertain

presage: standard unit headword | quell: standard unit headword
#11 Text Completion
The newly discovered fossil skeletons have the fins, scales, and other attributes of a giant fish, but also the telling anatomical traits of (i)______ creature, such as the beginnings of digits, protowrists, and joints resembling elbows on the forward fins. The specimen is a fish with features that (ii)______ the emergence of land animals and is thus a (iii)______ of amphibians, reptiles, dinosaurs, and mammals.The newly discovered fossil skeletons have the fins, scales, and other attributes of a giant fish, but also the telling anatomical traits of (i)______ creature, such as the beginnings of digits, protowrists, and joints resembling elbows on the forward fins. The specimen is a fish with features that (ii)______ the emergence of land animals and is thus a (iii)______ of amphibians, reptiles, dinosaurs, and mammals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　a transitional; anticipate; predecessor
derivativesupersede
derivative: standard unit headword | supersede: standard unit headword
#12 Text Completion
One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)______ . This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)______ , even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)______ . Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i)______ . This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii)______ , even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii)______ . Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　isolated; negligible; cause for concern
contentionnegligible
contention: standard unit headword | negligible: standard unit headword
#13 Text Completion
People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)______, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Once you raise the subject, the testimonies pour out, and, if your ears are tuned to it, you begin overhearing commute talk everywhere. People who are normally (ii)______ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)______ divulging the intimate details of their lives.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　gloat; circumspect; candid in
afflictiongrumble
affliction: standard unit headword | grumble: standard unit headword
#14 Text Completion
It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoarnerica. It must have been a (i)______ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii)______.It remains a mystery how Theobroma cacao, which scholars believe originated in lowland Amazonia, was introduced to tropical Mesoarnerica. It must have been a (i)______ process: cacao trees do not easily sprout from seed, need years to mature, and grow only in humid lowland forest. Moreover, in a tropical climate, cacao pods spoil quickly, rendering their transport from lowland Amazonia to tropical Mesoamerica in a single journey by foot or canoe (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　protracted; unlikely
renderspontaneous
render: standard unit headword | spontaneous: standard unit headword

#16 TC TRIPLE
Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom(ii) their literary models. But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been(i) the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Undeterred, theatrical managements ever since have used other people's literary repute to fill seats, though the adaptations still seldom(ii) their literary models. But in the1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　much abused by; equaled; confound
sharethrilling
share: standard unit headword | thrilling: standard unit headword
#17 SE
When automobiles became ____________ in Italy in the early 1950s, the country's ancient towns were inundated by vehicles, and their historic piazzas became, for a time, little more than parking lots.When automobiles became ____________ in Italy in the early 1950s, the country's ancient towns were inundated by vehicles, and their historic piazzas became, for a time, little more than parking lots.
答案：B, E　pervasive; ubiquitous
historic
historic: standard unit headword
#18 SE
Software makers typically write licensing agreements disclaiming responsibility for any negative consequences of using their products, but as computers become integrated into everything from cars to medical devices, that stance will become__________.Software makers typically write licensing agreements disclaiming responsibility for any negative consequences of using their products, but as computers become integrated into everything from cars to medical devices, that stance will become__________.
答案：B, D　untenable; indefensible
stance
stance: standard unit headword

Their (ii) the manifestations of modern science--railroads, radio, airplanes, and so forth--was potently expressed in language that often bordered on the mystical and magical.

Blank 2
答案：A, E　extolled; collective infatuation with.
extol

#20 TC TRIPLE
Reading Frankfurt's book. I worried that in the absence of specifie examples or nuaneed analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.Reading Frankfurt's book. I worried that in the absence of specifie examples or nuaneed analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i) in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii) these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii) that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　enthusiastically vilified; scapegoat; horror
gross
gross: standard unit headword

The earliest bridges were most likely __________ structures, like stones, fallen tree trunks, or hanging vines that provided a natural way for people to cross a stream.The earliest bridges were most likely __________ structures, like stones, fallen tree trunks, or hanging vines that provided a natural way for people to cross a stream.
答案：C　fortuitous
amorphous

答案：A, C　contentious; controversial
contentious

alacrity

答案：E, F　essential for; indispensable to

At first willing to reconcile herself to the imposture, she is eventually impelled to rebel by her innate sense of justice and her formidable skills as a gumshoe.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　autonomous; a figurehead; conform to

#33 TC DOUBLE
A skeptic is someone who does not believe a claim just because someone makes it but instead tests it against evidence. Sooner or later, if the evidence is solid, then the skeptic must acknowledge the claim's legitimacy. Skeptics are (i) ____________ deniers, who are ideologically committed to a position and (ii) ____________ in the face of evidence.A skeptic is someone who does not believe a claim just because someone makes it but instead tests it against evidence. Sooner or later, if the evidence is solid, then the skeptic must acknowledge the claim's legitimacy. Skeptics are (i) ____________ deniers, who are ideologically committed to a position and (ii) ____________ in the face of evidence.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　distinct from; intransigent
conscientious
conscientious: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　liminal; intermediate
archetypal

interrelated

答案：B, E　disparities; variances

答案：B, E　definitive; authoritative
authoritative

By now, however, his statements have taken on a different tone, becoming more (ii)_______, and consequently they are perceived as being more directed at coalition building.

Blank 2

答案：C, F　denounces; lambastes

#45 SE
Karin Knorr-Cetina argues that the production of technological knowledge is fundamentally __________: technological innovations arise within an "epistemic culture," that is, practitioners bonded by affinity who create and warrant emerging ideas.Karin Knorr-Cetina argues that the production of technological knowledge is fundamentally __________: technological innovations arise within an "epistemic culture," that is, practitioners bonded by affinity who create and warrant emerging ideas.
答案：B, F　collective; social
altruistic

答案：B, D　snag; hitch
hitch
hitch: standard unit headword

答案：B, E　scant; limited
dwindle
dwindle: standard unit headword

答案：C, D　undermined; impaired
neutralize
neutralize: standard unit headword
#49 Text Completion
To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that’s worth a cup of coffee.To pay for the extra spending under this international poverty plan, each American would have to contribute less than the cost of buying a premium cup of coffee once a week. But financial aid is not (i)______, and even if the funding recommended here were to (ii)______, the grandest objectives may well remain unfulfilled. Nonetheless, carefully targeted aid can reward responsible governments, (iii)______ individual initiative, and alleviate suffering. Many will think that’s worth a cup of coffee.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　a panacea; materialize; encourage
materialize
materialize: standard unit headword

Yet the true believers are (iii)______ , Critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)______ . The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)______ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)______ , Critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.

Blank 2

答案：A, F, H　amenable to refutation; upended; persistent

答案：B　incommensurable

But (ii) ____________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) ____________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) ____________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. Not all Native American languages have lexical classes corresponding to those of other languages: many have no adjectival category, and some have even brought the __________ of nouns and verbs into question.Not all Native American languages have lexical classes corresponding to those of other languages: many have no adjectival category, and some have even brought the __________ of nouns and verbs into question.
答案：D　universality
question
question: standard unit headword
#98 TC TRIPLE
The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.The advance of medical knowledge is not always (i) __________; on the contrary, it is often the result of (ii) __________. In the 1830s, groups of doctors began to question long-enshrined treatments such as bleeding, cupping, and purging, eventually discovering that many ailments were more effectively treated by nothing more than bed rest, nutrition, and observation. This news must have been (iii) __________ to those with a vested interest in the medical orthodoxy of the time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　cumulative; heretical departures; jarring
question
question: standard unit headword
#99 TC DOUBLE
The company CEO' s easygoing manner (i) his(ii) the job: he was in his office by dawn and hard at work till sunset.The company CEO' s easygoing manner (i) his(ii) the job: he was in his office by dawn and hard at work till sunset.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　belied; intensity on
intensity
intensity: standard unit headword
#100 TC SINGLE
The paintings of the 1960s Photo-Realists were meticulously rendered by artists who eschewed abstraction for realistic representation and thus achieved a precisely detailed, impersonal __________.The paintings of the 1960s Photo-Realists were meticulously rendered by artists who eschewed abstraction for realistic representation and thus achieved a precisely detailed, impersonal __________.
答案：A　verisimilitude
render
render: standard unit headword

His lackadaisical approach to this project belies his well- known reputation for .His lackadaisical approach to this project belies his well- known reputation for .
答案：B, C　assiduousness; industry

#102 TC TRIPLE
Until recently superhero comics were seen as irredeemably (i) ___________ ; they were widely disparaged for being so(ii) ___________ in conception and execution, and anyone who was a fan was given plenty of reasons to be (iii) ____________.Until recently superhero comics were seen as irredeemably (i) ___________ ; they were widely disparaged for being so(ii) ___________ in conception and execution, and anyone who was a fan was given plenty of reasons to be (iii) ____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　puerile; lowbrow; abashed

abash: standard unit headword

They share a generally (ii)________ vision of a future in which robots interact cooperatively with people in a wide variety of settings.Although fears of a dark, robot-controlled future are evident in the popular imagination, some scientists are more (i)________ about the impact that artificial intelligence will have in real life. They share a generally (ii)________ vision of a future in which robots interact cooperatively with people in a wide variety of settings.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　sanguine; propitious

答案：A　heterogeneous

Reading Frankfurt’s book, I worried that in the absence of specific examples or nuanced analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i)_______ in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. Of course, postmodernism is something of a (ii)_______ these days, and many readers may feel that no insult is too gross to heap on it. It arouses the same (iii)_______ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.Reading Frankfurt’s book, I worried that in the absence of specific examples or nuanced analysis, the unnamed postmodernists who are so (i)_______ in it might feel that they have been tarred by superficial and unsubstantiated generalizations. It arouses the same (iii)_______ that used to be aroused by idealism and skepticism.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　enthusiastically vilified; scapegoat; horror

Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism

#151 TC TRIPLE
Since scientific truths must be discovered, and since many, probably most, are far from (i) __________, futile investigations are (ii) __________. Thus, the path to the truth is decidedly a (iii) __________ one.Since scientific truths must be discovered, and since many, probably most, are far from (i) __________, futile investigations are (ii) __________. Thus, the path to the truth is decidedly a (iii) __________ one.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　intuitively obvious; inevitable; sinuous
negligible
negligible: standard unit headword
#152 Text Completion
The kilt has certainly had (i)______ history, outlawed as it was by Britain in 1746 as an emblem of nationalist subversion, then more recently (ii)______ from men’s to women’s apparel in a 2003 European Union survey, and ridiculed at various times for a wide variety of reasons.The kilt has certainly had (i)______ history, outlawed as it was by Britain in 1746 as an emblem of nationalist subversion, then more recently (ii)______ from men’s to women’s apparel in a 2003 European Union survey, and ridiculed at various times for a wide variety of reasons.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　a contentious; reclassified
contentious
contentious: standard unit headword
#153 TC DOUBLE
Galileo began to undermine humans' sense of self-importance a full five centuries ago, when he proved that Earth is not at the center of the universe, no matter how things look to us. Yet the tendency to judge the cosmos based on appearances and assumptions of our own (i) ____________ has proved (ii) ____________.Galileo began to undermine humans' sense of self-importance a full five centuries ago, when he proved that Earth is not at the center of the universe, no matter how things look to us. Yet the tendency to judge the cosmos based on appearances and assumptions of our own (i) ____________ has proved (ii) ____________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　primacy; tenacious
negligible
negligible: standard unit headword
#154 Sentence Equivalence
Many innovative breakthroughs come about when people venture beyond their own areas of expertise, because often it takes an outsider to ask the naïve question that may yield ______ solution.Many innovative breakthroughs come about when people venture beyond their own areas of expertise, because often it takes an outsider to ask the naïve question that may yield ______ solution.
答案：B, F　an unconventional; an original
question
question: standard unit headword
#155 SE
The author argues that Thomas Jefferson was able to acquire Louisiana from the French primarily through a sustained show of __________, though it seems likely that an amicable approach would have been more efficient in achieving that goal.The author argues that Thomas Jefferson was able to acquire Louisiana from the French primarily through a sustained show of __________, though it seems likely that an amicable approach would have been more efficient in achieving that goal.
答案：D, E　truculence; pugnacity
approach
approach: standard unit headword
#156 Text Completion
Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific work’s validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)______ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific work’s validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)______ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derives from; constitutes; disputed
derive
derive: standard unit headword
#157 TC TRIPLE
Ultimately, the young idealists (i)__________ the society they so fervently desired to reform: in their frustration, they reacted to the immediate situation by removing themselves from the postwar social and political arena, where the future of their country was being decided. In doing so, they (ii)_________ the salutary effects their rebellion should have (iii)____________ .Ultimately, the young idealists (i)__________ the society they so fervently desired to reform: in their frustration, they reacted to the immediate situation by removing themselves from the postwar social and political arena, where the future of their country was being decided. In doing so, they (ii)_________ the salutary effects their rebellion should have (iii)____________ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　did a great disservice to; nullified; begotten
supersede
supersede: standard unit headword
#158 TC DOUBLE
Determining how much water is on the Moon is challenging because most detections of water are (i) __________. One group of scientists, for instance, found that frozen volatiles-chemicals with the tendency to vaporize-lie on the lunar surface. That these compounds contain water, however, is (ii) __________.Determining how much water is on the Moon is challenging because most detections of water are (i) __________. One group of scientists, for instance, found that frozen volatiles-chemicals with the tendency to vaporize-lie on the lunar surface. That these compounds contain water, however, is (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　indirect; an inference
deceptive
deceptive: standard unit headword
#159 Text Completion
Shirky argues that the Internet (i)______ the need for hierarchical structures and the sluggish organizations that (ii)______ them: the Internet makes it possible to do things cheaply and efficiently on one’s own.Shirky argues that the Internet (i)______ the need for hierarchical structures and the sluggish organizations that (ii)______ them: the Internet makes it possible to do things cheaply and efficiently on one’s own.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　obviates; perpetuate
perpetuate

While recognizing that recent reports of cyberwarfare, phone-hacking scandals, and identity thefts have tended to accent the destructive connotation of the word, Sue Halpern maintains that “hacking” is such ______ term that its meaning nearly always derives from its context.While recognizing that recent reports of cyberwarfare, phone-hacking scandals, and identity thefts have tended to accent the destructive connotation of the word, Sue Halpern maintains that “hacking” is such ______ term that its meaning nearly always derives from its context.
答案：A, B　a generic; an inclusive

The chancellor once openly admired the relatively laissez-faire economic recipes of Britain and the United States but now defends the German way, in which she sees the free market’s sometimes destructive vicissitudes being _______ by consensus-building politics and a generous welfare state.The chancellor once openly admired the relatively laissez-faire economic recipes of Britain and the United States but now defends the German way, in which she sees the free market’s sometimes destructive vicissitudes being _______ by consensus-building politics and a generous welfare state.
答案：A, E　tempered; mitigated

Blank 2
答案：C, D　derivative; remarkably pioneering

(iii)______, accompanied by a certain aversion to risk taking, has pervaded its pages for a long time.Unlike most other serious journals, which drain money from their owners, the Review has long been (i)______. 答案：C, E　succumb to; yield to

Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)______ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)______ .Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)______ . Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)______ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　lawlike regulations; chaotic; self-perpetuating

Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)______ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　law-like regularities; chaotic; self-perpetuating
perpetuate
perpetuate: standard unit headword
#200 SE
Researchers who study pollinator behavior are increasingly interested in multicomponent floral signals, but they often focus on how individual components are learned by pollinators rather than on the benefit of signal ____________ per se.Researchers who study pollinator behavior are increasingly interested in multicomponent floral signals, but they often focus on how individual components are learned by pollinators rather than on the benefit of signal ____________ per se.
答案：A, F　complexity; intricacy
intensity
intensity: standard unit headword
#201 Sentence Equivalence
The tribes’ ______ a settled mode of living was derived from their long-standing traditions, which, though differing from one tribal group to another, always included a resistance to nomadic lifestyles.The tribes’ ______ a settled mode of living was derived from their long-standing traditions, which, though differing from one tribal group to another, always included a resistance to nomadic lifestyles.
答案：B, C　proclivity toward; predilection for
derive
derive: standard unit headword
#202 Text Completion
One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)______ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)______ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predated that event
debunk
debunk: standard unit headword
#203 SE
In his discussion of art, Ramachandran makes no distinction between the arousal value of a stimulus and its aesthetic value; he takes the emotional power of an artwork to be __________ that work's aesthetic quality.In his discussion of art, Ramachandran makes no distinction between the arousal value of a stimulus and its aesthetic value; he takes the emotional power of an artwork to be __________ that work's aesthetic quality.
答案：B, E　tantamount to; commensurate with
derive
derive: standard unit headword
#204 Sentence Equivalence
With regard to verity, neutrality, and transparency, nothing about the Internet makes it any different than Gutenberg’s printing press, which could serve ______ just as well as truth.With regard to verity, neutrality, and transparency, nothing about the Internet makes it any different than Gutenberg’s printing press, which could serve ______ just as well as truth.
答案：D, E　disinformation; mendacity
neutrality
neutrality: standard unit headword
#205 SE
Coastal waters of oceans are being starved of oxygen at rate, with vast stretches of water along the seafloor depleted of oxygen to the point that they can barely sustain marine life.Coastal waters of oceans are being starved of oxygen at rate, with vast stretches of water along the seafloor depleted of oxygen to the point that they can barely sustain marine life.
答案：B, E　an alarming; a disturbing
negligible
negligible: standard unit headword
#206 Text Completion
Even though the authors repeatedly ______ their own shrewdness, they show a remarkable credulousness toward far-fetched ideas such as carbon-eating trees and cloud-making machinery.Even though the authors repeatedly ______ their own shrewdness, they show a remarkable credulousness toward far-fetched ideas such as carbon-eating trees and cloud-making machinery.
答案：B　extol
extol
extol: standard unit headword

答案：A, F　ceded to; left to

question: standard unit headword

答案：A, C　annalist; chronicler

proponent: standard unit headword

(ii)______ by the (iii)______ of the volunteer, the Galaxy Zoo team was inspired to pursue lines of research they had never even imagined.Galaxy Zoo set a standard for citizen-scientist participation project. Zealous volunteers (i)______ the project’s organizers by classifying an entire catalog of galaxies years ahead of schedule. (ii)______ by the (iii)______ of the volunteer, the Galaxy Zoo team was inspired to pursue lines of research they had never even imagined.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　astonished; Buoyed; alacrity

The brain has become, for many people, ______ the biological machinations of the self, and the self-knowledge promised by neuroscience has ignited a hunger to understand how new findings weigh in on age-old questions.The brain has become, for many people, ______ the biological machinations of the self, and the self-knowledge promised by neuroscience has ignited a hunger to understand how new findings weigh in on age-old questions.
答案：A, B　tantamount to; synonymous with

question: standard unit headword

答案：A, E　upheaval; convulsion

historic: standard unit headword

答案：A, E　cooperate; collaborate

答案：D, F　visible; salient

conscientious: standard unit headword

Such a complete (ii)______ of responsibility on the part of the state can hardly be in the public’s interest.The building affairs minister rightly recognizes that the current planning system—under which the government controls every aspect of construction—creates disastrous developments, but she is wrong to propose the opposite: the wholesale (i)______ of the building market. Such a complete (ii)______ of responsibility on the part of the state can hardly be in the public’s interest.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　liberalization; abnegation

答案：A, C　prejudicial; tendentious

perfunctory: standard unit headword

答案：D, F　spurious; specious

Blank 2

答案：B, E　functional; utilitarian

spontaneous: standard unit headword

答案：C, F　specious; unfounded

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：B, F　offset; counterbalance

This cautious approach makes diplomatic sense, since attempts over the past 15 years to reach international agreements on patents have (ii)______ just such sensitive issues.

Blank 2

答案：A, D　capricious; cavalier

答案：C, D　sterile; unfruitful

Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)______ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)______ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)______ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)______ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　attend disproportionately to; responsiveness to; mask

aggregate: standard unit headword | mask: standard unit headword

We should be more ______ than we often are when making claims about antiquity—for example, the common statement, “the ancient Athenians invented democracy,” is simply not true when put like that.We should be more ______ than we often are when making claims about antiquity—for example, the common statement, “the ancient Athenians invented democracy,” is simply not true when put like that.
答案：C, F　precise; circumspect
skeptical
skeptical: standard unit headword

Partisans of literary realism sometimes think that because fantastical narratives do not abide by the laws of reality, they __________ all rules, though, in fact, the logic of fairy tales is often relentlessly rigorous.Partisans of literary realism sometimes think that because fantastical narratives do not abide by the laws of reality, they __________ all rules, though, in fact, the logic of fairy tales is often relentlessly rigorous.
答案：B, D　abjure; repudiate

答案：A, B　slippery; elusive

答案：E, F　heterogeneous; dissimilar

答案：A, B　tempered; moderated

答案：C, D　a decisive; an unmistakable

答案：A, C　contingent; circumstantial

答案：A, C　baseless; untenable

Communicating articulately is typically regarded as an aggressive, persuasive talent, but it can also be protective: it allows a certain ______ closeness, conveying proximity while actually maintaining distance.Communicating articulately is typically regarded as an aggressive, persuasive talent, but it can also be protective: it allows a certain ______ closeness, conveying proximity while actually maintaining distance.
答案：A, E　feigned; false

答案：B, C　conceded; acknowledged

question: standard unit headword

答案：C, F　restorative; tonic

答案：A, C　abused; misused

#247 Text Completion
Evidence has been accumulating since the 1930s that reducing an animals energy intake below its energy expenditure extends the life span and delays the (i)______ of age-related diseases in rats, dogs, fish, and monkeys. Such results have inspired thousands of people to (ii)______ in the hope of living longer, healthier lives. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)______ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.Evidence has been accumulating since the 1930s that reducing an animals energy intake below its energy expenditure extends the life span and delays the (i)______ of age-related diseases in rats, dogs, fish, and monkeys. Such results have inspired thousands of people to (ii)______ in the hope of living longer, healthier lives. They have also led to a search for drugs that (iii)______ the effects of calorie restriction without the pain of actually going on a diet.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　onset; put up with constant hunger; mimic

答案：B, E　waning; declining
intensify
intensify: standard unit headword

Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)______. Instead of (ii)______ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)______. You, not the author, are the master.Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)______. Instead of (ii)______ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)______. You, not the author, are the master.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　empowering; surrendering to; glean precisely what you want from it

approach: standard unit headword | question: standard unit headword

Although we no longer have to rely on salt to keep food from spoiling, our appetite for it is ______: most people eat much more salt than they need.Although we no longer have to rely on salt to keep food from spoiling, our appetite for it is ______: most people eat much more salt than they need.
答案：C, E　unabated; undiminished
inconstant
inconstant: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：A, D　a naïve; an unsophisticated

Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：A, C　porous; permeable

clog: standard unit headword
#255 Text Completion
Although so far the vast weight of evidence supports the contention that the products of agricultural biotechnology are environmentally (i)______, many people still find them (ii)______ unsettling.Although so far the vast weight of evidence supports the contention that the products of agricultural biotechnology are environmentally (i)______, many people still find them (ii)______ unsettling.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　sound; intrinsically
contention

#256 Sentence Equivalence
Smi’s analysis of climate change is even more ______ than that supplied by most well-informed writers, and thus deserves serious consideration.Smi’s analysis of climate change is even more ______ than that supplied by most well-informed writers, and thus deserves serious consideration.
答案：A, C　nuanced; complex
derivative
derivative: standard unit headword
#257 Text Completion
In its literature and its political discourse, the nation has created various narratives about itself that tend to ______ intractable social divisions in the interest of perpetuating a dubious myth of unity.In its literature and its political discourse, the nation has created various narratives about itself that tend to ______ intractable social divisions in the interest of perpetuating a dubious myth of unity.
答案：B　obscure
perpetuate
perpetuate: standard unit headword
#258 Sentence Equivalence
Some social scientists who begin their studies of small communities with the intention of remaining detached revise their initial approach on discovering that personal involvement with subjects can actually ______ the gathering of important data.Some social scientists who begin their studies of small communities with the intention of remaining detached revise their initial approach on discovering that personal involvement with subjects can actually ______ the gathering of important data.
答案：B, C　expedite; facilitate
approachintensify

#259 Text Completion
At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)______ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)______ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)______ in its effects on habitat.At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i)______ effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii)______ those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii)______ in its effects on habitat.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　significant; vastly exceed; unique
historic
historic: standard unit headword

To make waves large enough to be (i)______, the most (ii)______ events in the universe are required: supernova explosions, the formation of black holes, or the collision of stars. Even so, the effects are (iii) ______. The geometry changes so little that a distance of several kilometers changes by less than the diameter of a proton.Gravitational waves—ripples in the geometry of space-time—are analogous to electromagnetic waves. The challenge in trying to observe these waves directly is that they are extremely weak. To make waves large enough to be (i)______, the most (ii)______ events in the universe are required: supernova explosions, the formation of black holes, or the collision of stars. 答案：B, F　sympathy; petulant

答案：A　underscore

What remains is, at best, (ii)______ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander’s alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.The controversy about Alexander the Great’s personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i)______: all eyewitness accounts having perished. #365 Text Completion
When potentially disruptive change appears on the horizon, managers’ approach should be to assemble the capabilities to (i)______ that change before it affects the mainstream business. The (ii)______ to run two businesses, one with processes attuned to the existing business model and another geared toward the new model. While apparently onerous, this parallel operation is far less dangerous than either (iii)______ innovation irrevocably or remaining committed to tried-and-true methods that are threatened with obsolescence.When potentially disruptive change appears on the horizon, managers’ approach should be to assemble the capabilities to (i)______ that change before it affects the mainstream business. The (ii)______ to run two businesses, one with processes attuned to the existing business model and another geared toward the new model. While apparently onerous, this parallel operation is far less dangerous than either (iii)______ innovation irrevocably or remaining committed to tried-and-true methods that are threatened with obsolescence.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　handle; requirement is; jumping into
